Title: Valid login with correct credentials navigates to Dashboard
Preconditions: 1. App is accessible at https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Valid QA credentials available
Test_Steps: 1. Open browser and navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter valid email in the Email field 3. Enter valid password in the Password field 4. Click the Login button 5. Observe redirect
Expected_Result: User is redirected to the VWO Dashboard. Dashboard URL contains /dashboard or similar. Dashboard page title is visible. No error message shown.

Title: Invalid email format shows error message
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter an invalid email (no @ symbol) in the Email field 3. Enter any value in the Password field 4. Click Login 5. Observe response
Expected_Result: Login fails. An inline error message is displayed indicating invalid email or credentials. User remains on the login page.

Title: Email with missing domain shows error
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter email missing domain part in Email field 3. Enter password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login fails with error message. User remains on login page.

Title: Email with missing username shows error
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter email with only domain in Email field 3. Enter password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login fails with appropriate validation/error message. User remains on login page.

Title: Wrong password for valid email shows error
Preconditions: App is accessible. Valid email is registered in VWO.
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter a valid registered email 3. Enter an incorrect password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login fails. An error message is displayed (e.g. 'Invalid email or password'). User stays on login page. Password field is cleared.

Title: Unregistered email shows error
Preconditions: App is accessible. Email is NOT registered in VWO.
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter an email not registered with VWO 3. Enter any password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login fails with generic error (should not reveal whether email exists). User stays on login page.

Title: Empty email field shows validation
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Leave the Email field empty 3. Enter a password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login is blocked. A required-field validation message appears on or near the Email field. User stays on login page.

Title: Empty password field shows validation
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter a valid email 3. Leave the Password field empty 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login is blocked. A required-field validation message appears on or near the Password field. User stays on login page.

Title: Both fields empty shows validation
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Leave both Email and Password fields empty 3. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login is blocked. Validation messages appear for both fields. User stays on login page. No network request should be made.

Title: SQL injection in email field handled gracefully
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter SQL injection payload in Email field 3. Enter any value in Password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Application responds with a generic error message. No database error is exposed. No unintended authentication occurs. Page does not crash.

Title: SQL injection variant 2 in email field
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter admin comment injection in Email field 3. Enter password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Generic error shown. No bypass of authentication. No server error exposed.

Title: XSS script in email field is sanitized
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter XSS payload in Email field 3. Enter password 4. Click Login 5. Check if script executes
Expected_Result: No alert dialog appears. Input is sanitized or rejected. Generic error message shown. No script execution.

Title: XSS in password field is sanitized
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter valid email 3. Enter XSS payload in Password field 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: No script execution. Generic error or authentication failure shown. No XSS vulnerability exposed.

Title: Maximum length email (255 chars) is accepted or gracefully rejected
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter a 255-character email address in Email field 3. Enter password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Either: field accepts input and returns auth error (email not found), OR: field limits input to max length. No crash or server error.
Test_Data: Email: aaaa...aaa@test.com (255 total chars) | Password: TestPass@123

Title: Single-character password attempts handled
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter valid email 3. Enter single character in Password field 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Either: minimum-length validation message shown (if policy enforced on login form), OR: generic authentication failure shown. No crash.

Title: Login using Enter key submits form
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter valid email in Email field 3. Enter valid password in Password field 4. Press the Enter key (do NOT click the button)
Expected_Result: Form is submitted. User is authenticated and redirected to Dashboard. Behaviour identical to clicking the Login button.

Title: Tab key order follows logical field sequence
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Click into Email field 3. Press Tab 4. Observe which element receives focus 5. Press Tab again
Expected_Result: Focus moves: Email → Password → Login button (in order). No focus traps. Each focusable element is reachable via keyboard.

Title: Forgot Password link is visible and clickable
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Locate the Forgot Password link 3. Click the link
Expected_Result: User is redirected to the Forgot Password / password-reset page. No 404 or broken link.

Title: Error message clears when user retypes in field
Preconditions: User has attempted an invalid login and error message is visible.
Test_Steps: 1. Observe the error message after a failed login 2. Click into the Email field and type a character 3. Observe error message state
Expected_Result: Error message either disappears or the error indicator on the field clears as the user begins retyping.

Title: Email field displays correct placeholder text
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Observe the Email input field before typing
Expected_Result: Email field displays a descriptive placeholder (e.g. 'Enter your email' or 'Email address'). Placeholder disappears when user clicks field.

Title: Password characters are masked by default
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Click Password field 3. Type a password
Expected_Result: Characters appear as dots or asterisks. Password is not visible as plain text.

Title: Email field accepts valid email with subdomain
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter valid email with subdomain 3. Enter password 4. Click Login
Expected_Result: Login attempt is made (authentication success/failure based on credentials). No format validation error for subdomain emails.

Title: Copy-paste into Password field works correctly
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Copy a password string from clipboard 3. Paste into the Password field 4. Enter valid email 5. Click Login
Expected_Result: Password is accepted via paste. Login proceeds normally (success or auth failure based on credentials). No paste-blocking behaviour.

Title: Dashboard page loads after successful login
Preconditions: Valid credentials are available. User is on the login page.
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to https://app.vwo.com/ 2. Enter valid email and password 3. Click Login 4. Wait for page load 5. Verify URL and page title
Expected_Result: URL changes from login URL to dashboard URL. Dashboard page title contains 'VWO' or 'Dashboard'. Page fully loads within 2 seconds (per NFR).

Title: Main navigation menu is visible on Dashboard
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Dashboard page.
Test_Steps: 1. After successful login observe the left or top navigation 2. Check for main menu items (e.g. Testing, Insights, Personalize, etc.)
Expected_Result: Navigation menu is present and visible. At minimum 3 navigation items are displayed. Items are clickable.

Title: User account / profile menu is visible on Dashboard
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Dashboard page.
Test_Steps: 1. After successful login look for user profile indicator (avatar / initials / name) 2. Click on the profile indicator 3. Check dropdown/menu options
Expected_Result: Profile icon or name is visible in the header. Clicking opens a dropdown with options (e.g. Account Settings, Logout).

Title: VWO logo and branding are visible on Dashboard
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Dashboard page.
Test_Steps: 1. After successful login observe the header 2. Check for VWO logo
Expected_Result: VWO logo is visible in the header/sidebar. Logo image loads without error (no broken image).

Title: Session persists after page refresh
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Dashboard page.
Test_Steps: 1. After successful login note the Dashboard URL 2. Press F5 / Ctrl+R to refresh the page 3. Observe if user remains logged in
Expected_Result: User remains authenticated. Dashboard reloads without redirecting to login. No session expiry during an active session.

Title: Logout clears session and redirects to login
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the Dashboard page.
Test_Steps: 1. Click the User profile menu 2. Click Logout 3. Observe redirect 4. Press browser Back button
Expected_Result: User is redirected to the login page after logout. Pressing Back does NOT re-enter the dashboard (session is invalidated). Login page is shown.
